title: multi-include
description: A multi-file \include project: main.tex assembles chap1.tex + chap2.tex and prints a \tableofcontents. Everything is BASE LaTeX (report.cls + \include + \tableofcontents + \label/\ref), all in the core tier, so no academic package is scanned and the doc stays core-only (bundlesLoaded=['core'], resolution=none). Chapter 1 forward-\ref's chapter 2, so pass 1 has an undefined reference and LaTeX prints 'Rerun to get cross-references right'; the §5.3 auto-rerun loop runs a SECOND pass that resolves it — phases=['engine','engine'] pins exactly the two passes. The pinned diagnostics are the pass-1 undefined-reference warnings the multi-pass transcript retains (like the bib-cite seed): a real parser exercise with file/line attribution across the \included chap1.tex.

diagnostics.message: LaTeX Warning: Reference `chap:results' on page 2 undefined on input line 5.
LaTeX Warning: There were undefined references.
LaTeX Warning: Label(s) may have changed. Rerun to get cross-references right.
